source.title: Our World in Data — Crop Yields
source.note: Lettuce — yield_kg_ha: FAO global average used for yield value.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Lettuce — water_per_kg: 130 L/kg used (sourced from WFN online crop database). Report 47 Table A1 reports 237 L/kg. The discrepancy reflects different methodologies: Report 47 uses a global production-weighted virtual water model; the WFN online calculator (130 L/kg) applies simplified blue+green accounting. Neither is clearly wrong; the lower figure may reflect the dominant role of intensive irrigation-efficient greenhouse production (particularly in China). Value flagged but not corrected — difference is within methodological range.

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Lettuce — soil_erosion: ~3 t/ha/yr estimated for intensive lettuce production; short growing cycle with frequent tillage increases erosion risk; value consistent with P&N 2018 LCA boundary data for salad crops.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Lettuce — pesticide_kg_ha: 1.4 kg/ha (USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey; weighted average of active ingredient applications across California and Arizona lettuce production; 2018–2022 average).

source.title: FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Lettuce — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~120 kg/ha (intensive leafy vegetable; high nitrogen demand for rapid leaf growth).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Lettuce — emissions_per_kg: ~0.5 kg CO2e/kg (field-grown; P&N 2018 'other vegetables' global median covers leafy salad crops; value covers farm-gate GHG including fertilizer N2O, farm energy, and minor land use component).

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Lettuce — emissions_per_kg: 0.53 kg CO2e/kg, from OWID chart 'Other Vegetables' category sourcing P&N 2018; consistent with multi-study LCA range of 0.4–0.6 kg CO2e/kg for field-grown lettuce.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Lettuce — tillage_events_per_year: ~3 per year (multiple lettuce crops per year common in California and Arizona; each cycle requires full bed preparation including discing and listing; P&N 2018 LCA boundary data).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Lettuce — co2_capture_kg_ha_yr: ~800 kg CO2/ha/yr; short-season crop with low biomass accumulation; estimate based on above-ground carbon fixation for salad crops (P&N 2018 LCA framework).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Lettuce — imidacloprid: 0.05 kg/ha; permethrin: 0.07 kg/ha; pendimethalin: 0.18 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Lettuce — imidacloprid: 0.05 kg/ha; permethrin: 0.07 kg/ha; pendimethalin: 0.18 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Lettuce — pendimethalin: 0.18 kg/ha; pre-emergent herbicide widely used on head and leaf lettuce for broadleaf and grass weed control; USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: Our World in Data — Crop Yields
source.note: Spinach — yield_kg_ha: 20,000 kg/ha (intensive irrigated vegetable production; China accounts for ~90% of global spinach output; FAO 2020–2022).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Spinach — water_per_kg: 305 L/kg (global average; short-cycle leafy crop with low total water input).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Spinach — soil_erosion: ~0.5 t/ha/yr; low-erosion crop due to dense canopy cover and short production cycle; estimate consistent with P&N 2018 LCA boundary data for low-input leafy vegetables.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Spinach — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~120 kg/ha (intensive leafy vegetable; high nitrogen demand for rapid leaf growth).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Spinach — emissions_per_kg: 0.47 kg CO2-eq/kg (low-input leafy vegetable; P&N 2018).

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Spinach — emissions_per_kg: 0.53 kg CO2e/kg from OWID 'Other Vegetables' category (P&N 2018 data); corroborates the P&N 2018 direct value of 0.47 kg CO2e/kg; both within the expected 0.3–0.6 kg CO2e/kg range for low-input leafy crops.

source.title: FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Spinach — tillage_events_per_year: ~3 per year; multiple short-cycle crops grown annually; each planting requires seedbed preparation; FAO vegetable production guidelines.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Spinach — co2_capture_kg_ha_yr: ~1000 kg CO2/ha/yr; dense rapid-growth canopy with high leaf area index supports moderate carbon fixation over multiple short cycles per year; P&N 2018 LCA framework.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Spinach — permethrin: 0.09 kg/ha; imidacloprid: 0.07 kg/ha; mancozeb: 0.15 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Spinach — permethrin: 0.09 kg/ha; imidacloprid: 0.07 kg/ha; mancozeb: 0.15 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Spinach — mancozeb: 0.15 kg/ha; dithiocarbamate fungicide applied for downy mildew (Peronospora farinosa) control, the primary fungal disease of spinach; USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average).

source.title: Our World in Data — Crop Yields
source.note: Kale — yield_kg_ha: 15,000 kg/ha (estimate derived from brassica family yields; FAO does not report kale separately; value consistent with broccoli/cabbage leaf yields; confidence 3).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Kale — water_per_kg: 237 L/kg (global average; cool-season leafy brassica, lower water demand than most crops).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Kale — soil_erosion: ~0.5 t/ha/yr; cool-season brassica with long growing season and good canopy cover; low erosion risk relative to bare-soil crops; P&N 2018 LCA boundary data for other brassicas.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Kale — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~100 kg/ha (brassica; moderate nitrogen demand; similar to other leafy brassicas).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Kale — emissions_per_kg: 0.49 kg CO2-eq/kg (brassica leafy vegetable; P&N 2018 'other brassicas').

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Kale — emissions_per_kg: 0.51 kg CO2e/kg from OWID 'Brassicas' category (P&N 2018 data); closely corroborates the P&N 2018 direct value of 0.49 kg CO2e/kg for kale as a leafy brassica; within the known-correct brassica range of 0.4–0.9 kg CO2e/kg.

source.title: FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Kale — tillage_events_per_year: ~3 per year; typically 1–2 crops per year with bed preparation between cycles; some operations include mid-season cultivation for weed control; FAO vegetable production guidelines.

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Kale — co2_capture_kg_ha_yr: ~1200 kg CO2/ha/yr; longer growing season and larger plant biomass than short-cycle leafy crops; higher canopy carbon fixation relative to lettuce and spinach; P&N 2018 LCA framework for brassica crops.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Kale — permethrin: 0.12 kg/ha; imidacloprid: 0.05 kg/ha; carbaryl: 0.10 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average; kale grouped with other brassica greens).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Kale — permethrin: 0.12 kg/ha; imidacloprid: 0.05 kg/ha; carbaryl: 0.10 kg/ha. Values from USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average; kale grouped with other brassica greens).

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
source.note: Kale — carbaryl: 0.10 kg/ha; carbamate insecticide applied for Lepidoptera control (imported cabbageworm, diamondback moth, cabbage looper) on brassica greens; USDA NASS Vegetables Chemical Use Survey (2018–2022 average; kale grouped with other brassica greens).
